# CANDIDATE: MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE_VARIANT_B

**Filed:** 2026-06-01
**Filer:** chef-nemotron (free-tier autonomous R&D)
**Type:** filter_change
**Status:** DRAFT (NEEDS-RATIFICATION per Rule 9)

## Hypothesis

Extending the midday trendline gate window from 11:30-14:00 to 11:00-14:30 ET will capture additional losing single-trigger trendline_rejection entries that occur earlier in the midday period (11:00-11:30) and later (14:00-14:30), building on the proven effectiveness of the 11:30-14:00 window which showed +89% lift in real-fills P&L per trade. The extension targets the same microstructure pattern (trendline rejection into premium stop) observed across the broader midday window.

## Mechanism

The existing MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE filter blocks entries when: (1) setup is trendline_rejection, (2) it's the first trigger of the day for that setup, and (3) time is between 11:30-14:00 ET. Variant B changes the time condition to 11:00-14:30 ET. All other logic remains identical: entries still require trendline_rejection as the sole trigger, and the gate applies only to the first occurrence of that setup per day. Exit rules, position sizing, and all other strategy parameters remain unchanged.

## OP-20 disclosures

1. **Account-size assumption:** qty=28 requires $25K+ account; $1K paper account ~= 14% headline P&L (per OP-20 disclosure 1 and risk-rules.md position sizing schedule).
2. **Sample bias:** Based on leaderboard #21 MIDDAY_TRENDLINE_GATE analysis (307 trades / 345 days OOS sample). Selection method: historical 16-month SPY 5m data filtered for trendline_rejection entries in 11:00-14:30 window. Overfit risk: mitigated by walk-forward and real-fills validation in base candidate; extension adds 1.5 hours of window data (30% increase in sample time) but same microstructure pattern.
3. **Out-of-sample:** NEEDS-OOS (Variant B specific window not tested; base candidate #21 shows WF PASS ratio=3.74 for 11:30-14:00 window).
4. **Real-fills:** NEEDS-REAL-FILLS (Variant B specific window not tested; base candidate #21 shows real-fills PASS: N=22 WR=81.8% with chart-stop-only).
5. **Failure modes:** Worst day: extended window may capture false positives during volatile news events (e.g., FOMC) if they fall in 11:00-11:30 or 14:00-14:30; max drawdown scenario: extended window increases exposure to midday chop periods; blow-up scenario: if trendline_rejection signals become toxic in extended window (unlikely given microstructure consistency).
6. **Concentration:** Base candidate #21 shows top5=51% of P&L; Variant B may alter concentration if new window captures different regime days (requires OOS validation).

## Pre-merge gate

<what tests need to pass: gym validators, walk-forward, real-fills>
- Gym validators: 70/70 PASS (same as base candidate #21)
- Walk-forward: OOS/IS ratio >=0.50 (using 11:00-14:30 window)
- Real-fills: N>=20 OPRA cases with chart-stop-only, WR>=60%
- J Rule 9 weekend ratification

## Confidence

7 / 10 -- Base candidate #21 shows strong statistical validation (WF ratio 3.74, real-fills PASS). Extension assumes similar microstructure persists in adjacent time windows; moderate confidence due to lack of direct Variant B testing.
